data.content:
### Configure Ready Command in e2b.toml

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox-template/ready-cmd/page.mdx

This shows how to configure the ready command within the `e2b.toml` file. The `ready_cmd` field accepts a string representing the command to be executed for readiness checks.

```toml
# This is a config for E2B sandbox template
template_id = "1wdqsf9le9gk21ztb4mo"
dockerfile = "e2b.Dockerfile"
template_name = "my-agent-sandbox"
ready_cmd = "<your-ready-command>"
```

--------------------------------

### Python Method to Get Available Formats from Result

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.1.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieves all available MIME types for the data contained within a Result object. This helps in determining how the data can be rendered.

```python
def formats() -> Iterable[str]

```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Get Available Result Formats

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.5.0/messaging/page.mdx

The `formats()` method returns an array of strings, indicating all the available formats in which the result data can be represented. This is useful for understanding the output capabilities of the sandbox.

```typescript
formats(): string[]
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Sandbox Template Start Command (e2b.toml)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox-template/start-cmd/page.mdx

Defines the start command within the sandbox template's configuration file (`e2b.toml`). This allows for persistent configuration of the command that will run when the sandbox is instantiated.

```toml
# This is a config for E2B sandbox template
template_id = "1wdqsf9le9gk21ztb4mo"
dockerfile = "e2b.Dockerfile"
template_name = "my-agent-sandbox"
start_cmd = "<your-start-command>"
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template (e2b CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.0/template/page.mdx

Deletes a sandbox template and its associated configuration file (e2b.toml). It allows for interactive selection and confirmation skipping.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

# Example to delete interactively and skip confirmation:
e2b template delete -s -y
```

--------------------------------

### Generic Screenshot Capture in E2B Sandbox (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-python-sdk/v1.6.5/sandbox/page.mdx

Takes a screenshot of the sandbox in the specified format ('bytes', 'blob', or 'stream'). It defaults to returning 'bytes'. This function provides flexibility in how screenshot data is retrieved.

```python
def screenshot(format: Literal["bytes", "stream"] = "bytes")

```

--------------------------------

### Get Download URL for a File (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.4/sandbox/page.mdx

This code shows how to retrieve a URL for downloading a specific file from the sandbox. The `downloadUrl` method requires the path to the file within the sandbox and returns a string representing the download URL.

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
const downloadURL = sandbox.downloadUrl('/home/user/my_file.txt')
```

--------------------------------

### Migrate E2B Template to SDK Format using E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.3/template/page.mdx

Migrates e2b.Dockerfile and e2b.toml to the new Template SDK format. No specific options are detailed beyond the command itself.

```bash
e2b template migrate [options]
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Get Available Result Formats

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.0.4/messaging/page.mdx

Retrieves a list of all available formats for a given result. This method is useful for understanding the different ways data can be represented and accessed.

```typescript
/**
 * Returns all the formats available for the result.
 * @returns {string[]} Array of strings representing the formats available for the result.
 */
formats(): string[]
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.1/template/page.mdx

Deletes a sandbox template and its associated e2b.toml configuration file. It supports specifying the path, configuration file, and allows for interactive selection or skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  -y, --yes: skip manual delete confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### Download Data from Sandbox using Pre-signed URLs

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/filesystem/download/page.mdx

This method generates a secure, time-limited URL to download files from the sandbox, allowing access from unauthorized environments like a browser. It requires creating a sandbox with `secure: true`. The input is the file path and an optional expiration time, and the output is a public URL to the file.

```javascript
import fs from 'fs'
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

// Start a secured sandbox (all operations must be authorized by default)
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create(template, { secure: true })

// Create a pre-signed URL for file download with a 10 second expiration
const publicUrl = await sandbox.downloadUrl(
  'demo.txt', {
    useSignatureExpiration: 10_000, // optional
  },
)

// Download a file with a pre-signed URL (this can be used in any environment, such as a browser)
const res = await fetch(publicUrl)
const content = await res.text()
```

```python
from e2b import Sandbox

# Start a secured sandbox (all operations must be authorized by default)
sandbox = Sandbox.create(timeout=12_000, secure=True)

# Create a pre-signed URL for file download with a 10 second expiration
# The user only has to visit the URL to download the file, this also works in a browser.
signed_url = sbx.download_url(path="demo.txt", user="user", use_signature_expiration=10_000)
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.0.1/template/page.mdx

Deletes a specified sandbox template and its associated e2b.toml configuration file. Options include specifying the path, selecting from an interactive list, and skipping confirmation prompts.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
 - `--config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
 - `-s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list
 - `-t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
 - `-y, --yes: skip manual delete confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.5.1/template/page.mdx

Deletes a specified sandbox template and its associated e2b.toml configuration file. It offers options to select a template interactively, skip confirmation, or specify a team ID.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>`: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - `--config <e2b-toml>`: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - `-s, --select`: select sandbox template from interactive list 
 - `-t, --team <team-id>`: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - `-y, --yes`: skip manual delete confirmation 
```

--------------------------------

### Get Download URL for a File

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.4/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieves a URL to download a specific file from the sandbox. This function takes the file path as input and returns a string representing the download URL.

```typescript
const downloadUrl = sandbox.downloadUrl('/path/to/file')
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.5.2/template/page.mdx

Deletes a sandbox template and its associated e2b.toml configuration file. Options include specifying the path, configuration file, selecting from a list, specifying the team ID, and skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - `--config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - `-s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list 
 - `-t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - `-y, --yes: skip manual delete confirmation 
```

--------------------------------

### Get URL for uploading to sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.5/sandbox/page.mdx

Provides a URL endpoint to upload files into the sandbox. A POST request with the file as `multipart/form-data` should be sent to this URL. Optionally, a destination path can be specified.

```typescript
const uploadUrl = sandbox.uploadUrl('/path/to/destination/');
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.0/template/page.mdx

Deletes a sandbox template and its associated e2b.toml configuration file. It supports specifying the template path, configuration file, selecting from an interactive list, and confirming deletion.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]
```

--------------------------------

### Get E2B Sandbox File Download URL

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.2/sandbox/page.mdx

This snippet illustrates how to obtain a pre-signed URL for downloading a specific file from the sandbox using the `downloadUrl()` method. This allows external access to files generated or stored within the sandbox.

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
const downloadLink = await sandbox.downloadUrl('/path/to/your/file.txt')
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.7.0/template/page.mdx

Removes a sandbox template and its associated e2b.toml configuration file. Supports specifying the path, configuration file, team ID, and options to select templates interactively or skip confirmation.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

Options:
 - -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list 
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - -y, --yes: skip manual delete confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve E2B Sandbox Information

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Fetches detailed information about the sandbox, including its ID, template, metadata, and operational times. This method helps in identifying and monitoring sandbox instances. Optional `requestTimeoutMs` can be provided.

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
const info = await sandbox.getInfo()
```

--------------------------------

### Download File URL

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.10.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieves a pre-signed URL to download a file from the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## Download File URL

### Description
Get the URL to download a file from the sandbox.

### Method
`sandbox.downloadUrl(path: string, opts?: SandboxUrlOpts): Promise<string>`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to the file in the sandbox.
- **opts** (SandboxUrlOpts) - Optional - Download URL options.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **string** - URL for downloading file.
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.9.1/template/page.mdx

Deletes a specified sandbox template and its associated e2b.toml configuration file. Options include specifying the root path, config file, selecting from a list, associating with a team, and skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>`: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
 - `--config <e2b-toml>`: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
 - `-s, --select`: select sandbox template from interactive list
 - `-t, --team <team-id>`: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
 - `-y, --yes`: skip manual delete confirmation
```
